sourceMovie.title: The Cable Guy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Cable Guy for the same reason it broke through in 1996: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ben Stiller keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Modern Times
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Cable Guy clicked for you, Modern Times (1936) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Cable Guy?
faq.answer: Try Modern Times, The King of Comedy, Bottle Rocket—each shares DNA with The Cable Guy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Cable Guy?
faq.answer: The Cable Guy (1996) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Cable Guy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
